title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Washington Heights, Manhattan
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Washington Heights, Manhattan. Humane, effective solutions for homes and buildings. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Washington Heights residents facing bat infestations need fast, humane, and effective solutions to protect their homes and families. Our professional bat removal and exclusion services are specifically designed for the unique architectural characteristics of Washington Heights buildings, from pre-war walk-ups to Fort Tryon Park-area homes. We provide complete bat control services with pricing ranging from $900 to $2,500 depending on the severity of the infestation and building size.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has been serving Washington Heights for years with specialized knowledge of local building structures and bat behavior in Upper Manhattan. Our licensed technicians use humane exclusion methods that comply with all NYC regulations while providing guaranteed results and follow-up inspections to ensure your home remains bat-free.
localContext: Washington Heights' historic apartment buildings, townhouses near Fort Tryon Park, and homes along the Hudson River often feature the aging rooflines, chimneys, and ventilation systems that attract roosting bats. The neighborhood's proximity to green spaces like Highbridge Park and Inwood Hill Park makes it particularly susceptible to bat activity, especially during migration seasons.

faqs.question: Why are bats common in Washington Heights buildings?
faqs.answer: Washington Heights has many pre-war buildings with older architecture that develops gaps in rooflines, chimneys, and siding over time. Combined with the neighborhood's proximity to Fort Tryon Park and the Hudson River, these structures provide ideal roosting spots for bats seeking shelter. Attics, wall voids, and unused chimneys are particularly attractive to bat colonies.

faqs.question: What does the bat removal process involve?
faqs.answer: Our bat removal process begins with a thorough inspection to identify entry points and roosting areas. We then install one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to exit but not re-enter, ensuring humane removal without harming the animals. After all bats have left, we seal all entry points and provide sanitation services to remove droppings and contamination. The entire process typically takes 3-7 days depending on colony size.

faqs.question: Is bat removal in Washington Heights regulated by NYC?
faqs.answer: Yes, bat removal in NYC must follow strict guidelines as many bat species are protected. Our licensed technicians are fully trained in legal, humane exclusion methods that comply with all New York State and NYC regulations. We never use harmful extermination methods and time our services to avoid maternity season (May-August) when flightless baby bats may be present.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Washington Heights?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Washington Heights typically range from $900 to $2,500 depending on factors like colony size, number of entry points, building height and accessibility, and extent of sanitation needed. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates with no hidden fees. Most residential jobs in Washington Heights fall in the $1,200-$1,800 range for complete exclusion and sealing services.
